channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Barges are bringing cranes to Baltimore to help remove bridge wreckage and open shipping route
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — Massive barges carrying cranes streamed toward Baltimore on Thursday to begin the challenging work of removing twisted metal and concrete in an attempt to open a key shipping route blocked by the wreckage of the Francis Scott Key Bridge. U.S.
item.dc:creator: LEA SKENE and BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Amid wars and White House race, celebs weigh risks of speaking out
item.description: Celebrities went all in during the 2020 and 2016 White House races, but they are proceeding with more caution this election cycle.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, ABC 4

item.title: West Point cadet, 21, found dead in Florida during spring break trip
item.description: A West Point cadet was found dead this week following what officials believe to be an accidental drowning, the United States Military Academy has confirmed.
item.dc:creator: Phil Mayer, ABC 4

item.title: Universal's 'How To Train Your Dragon' land revealed for Epic Universe
item.description: How to Train Your Dragon – Isle of Berk will be one of five worlds that will be featured in the all-new Universal Epic Universe set to open in 2025.
item.dc:creator: Scott Gustin, ABC 4

item.title: Biden fundraiser with Obama and Clinton nets a record high $25 million, the campaign says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A fundraiser for President Joe Biden on Thursday in New York City that also stars Barack Obama and Bill Clinton is raising a whopping $25 million, setting a record for the biggest haul for a political event, his campaign said.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: French lawmakers condemn 'bloody and murderous' 1961 massacre of Algerian protesters
item.description: PARIS (AP) — French lawmakers on Thursday condemned an infamous 1961 police crackdown on Algerian protesters in Paris as a “bloody and murderous repression,” marking another step in the country's recognition of the massacre that authorities sought to cover up for decades.
item.dc:creator: AP, PHL 17

item.title: Putin says he won't start a war with NATO but Western bases hosting Ukraine F-16s would be targets
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russian President Vladimir Putin scoffed at the possibility of his country launching an attack on a NATO member, calling it “sheer nonsense,” but warned that any Western air base hosting U.S.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV and BARRY HATTON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Philippine president warns of countermeasures in response to Chinese aggression at sea
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — The Philippine president said Thursday that his government would take action against what he called dangerous attacks by the Chinese coast guard and suspected militia ships in the disputed South China Sea, saying “Filipinos do not yield.” Ferdinand Marcos Jr.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Don't touch! These fuzzy bugs are taking over Florida
item.description: Springtime ushers in an influx of an infamous Florida pest. They appear to be everywhere, but be careful not to touch them.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, The Hill

item.title: Bidens announce theme for Easter Egg Roll on White House lawn
item.description: The White House announced Thursday that the upcoming 2024 Easter Egg Roll, hosted by first lady Jill Biden, will be themed around "EGGucation" and is expecting over 40,000 attendees.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: VP Harris says US agencies must show their AI tools aren't harming people's safety or rights
item.description: U.S. federal agencies must show that their artificial intelligence tools aren't harming the public, or stop using them, under new rules unveiled by the White House on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Criminal profiling: The techniques used by police to catch dangerous offenders
item.description: Criminal profiling is an investigative technique used by law enforcement to identify offenders based on distinct behavioral characteristics and motives.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Texas lawmaker asks governor declare emergency due to 'water crisis'
item.description: A South Texas lawmaker on Wednesday asked Gov. Greg Abbott to declare an emergency due to what he calls a water "crisis" on the border in the Rio Grande Valley.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: King Charles III in Easter message calls for kindness after cancer diagnosis
item.description: King Charles III shared a message Thursday calling for kindness and friendship “in a time of need,” after he and Kate, the Princess of Wales, were both diagnosed with cancer.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KRON4

item.title: What to know about the cargo ship Dali, a mid-sized ocean monster that took down a Baltimore bridge
item.description: Here's what to know about the cargo ship Dali that crashed into Baltimore's Francis Scott Key Bridge, causing it to collapse and leaving six bridge construction workers presumed dead.
item.dc:creator: NICK PERRY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US changes how it categorizes people by race and ethnicity. It's the first revision in 27 years
item.description: For the first time in 27 years, the U.S. government is changing how it categorizes people by race and ethnicity.
item.dc:creator: Mike Schneider, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Jane Goodall to celebrate 90th birthday with talks on urgency of environmental action
item.description: Dr. Jane Goodall, a renowned British primatologist and conservationist, is approaching her 90th birthday and plans to celebrate with a series of environmental talks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Venezuelans are increasingly stuck in Mexico, explaining drop in illegal crossings to US
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Venezuelan migrants often have a quick answer when asked to name the most difficult stretch of their eight-country journey to the U.S. border, and it's not the dayslong jungle trek through Colombia and Panama with its venomous vipers, giant spiders and scorpions. It's Mexico.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER SHERMAN, ELLIOT SPAGAT and VALERIE GONZALEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: iPhone flooded with notifications? Beware new hack trick
item.description: A mess of notifications is nothing new on the iPhone. But this particular type of hack attack that abuses them is. It's called "MFA Bombing," the latest way hackers attempt to access your Apple Account. Follow Tech Reporter Rich DeMuro for more tech tips, news and reviews.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, KTLA 5

item.title: Toxic toads that can kill pets in minutes are breeding in Florida
item.description: The toads, ranging between 6 and 9 inches in length, release a milky toxin that sticks in pets' mouths.
item.dc:creator: Katlyn Brieskorn, NewsNation

item.title: Lego tells police to stop hiding suspects' faces with toy heads
item.description: Lego has since "respectfully asked" the department to "refrain from using their intellectual property in our social media content.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, NewsNation

item.title: New 2,000-strong migrant caravan heading for US
item.description: JUAREZ, Mexico (Border Report) – Church-run border shelters are preparing for the arrival of additional large groups of migrants from southern Mexico. Several hundred foreign nationals have already set up camp behind a convenience store in Chihuahua City some 230 miles south of El Paso, Texas.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, NewsNation

item.title: Biden is announcing a new rule to protect consumers who purchase short-term health insurance plans
item.description: President Joe Biden on Thursday announced new steps to protect consumers who buy short-term health insurance plans that critics say amount to junk. A new rule finalized by the Democratic president's administration will limit these plans to just three months.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Still climbing: Powerball jackpot increases to $935 million
item.description: (AP) — The Powerball jackpot increased to an estimated $935 million after no one matched the six numbers drawn Wednesday night. The numbers selected were: 37, 46, 57, 60, 66 and the Powerball 8.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: TIMELINE: Voyage data recorder details moments before cargo ship crashed into Baltimore bridge
item.description: The National Transportation Safety Board detailed during a Wednesday night news conference what is believed to have happened in the moments before a cargo ship crashed into Baltimore's Francis Scott Key Bridge, sending the bridge collapsing into the water.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Accettulla, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Ex-Trump lawyer Eastman should lose state law license for efforts to overturn election, judge says
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — A judge has recommended that conservative attorney John Eastman lose his California law license over his efforts to keep former President Donald Trump in power after the 2020 election.
item.dc:creator: STEFANIE DAZIO and CHRISTOPHER WEBER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Hunter Biden's lawyers push California judge to toss out tax charges
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Attorneys for Hunter Biden asked a judge Wednesday to toss out the tax case accusing him of a four-year scheme to avoid paying $1.4 million in taxes while living an extravagant lifestyle.
item.dc:creator: STEFANIE DAZIO and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: 4 dead and 7 injured in Illinois stabbings; suspect in custody: police
item.description: Police in Illinois said 4 people, including a mail carrier, are dead and 7 injured after a series of attacks by a suspect in a southeast Rockford neighborhood Wednesday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: John Clark, KTLA 5

item.title: Disney concept art, rare memorabilia available in upcoming auction
item.description: The collection will spotlight work from Marc and Alice Davis, former Disney Imagineers who worked on classic Disney movies and attractions.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Baltimore bridge workers' presumed deaths highlight dangers of essential jobs
item.description: The presumed deaths of six road workers on the Francis Scott Key Bridge in Baltimore are shining a spotlight on the risks associated with essential jobs, especially for low-income and immigrant workers.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, Fox 5

item.title: Eclipse 2024: Will it affect your plants?
item.description: Researchers in the past have observed how animals react to a total solar eclipse, but how would it affect plants?
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, Fox 5

item.title: State Department offers $10 million reward for info on UnitedHealthcare hackers
item.description: The State Department announced Wednesday it would provide a $10 million reward for information about the Blackcat ransomware group that hacked UnitedHealth Group in February.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: Baltimore bridge collapse: Ships carrying cars and heavy equipment need to find a new harbor
item.description: Manufacturers and shippers are scrambling to figure out where they can load or unload cargo while the main operations of the Port of Baltimore remain shut down due to Tuesday's deadly collapse of the Francis Scott Key Bridge.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 2 bodies recovered as search continues for missing workers after Baltimore bridge collapse
item.description: The workers lost in the Baltimore bridge collapse all hailed from Mexico or Central America before they settled in the Maryland area.
item.dc:creator: Claudio Escalón, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Disney, DeSantis-backed board reach settlement agreement
item.description: Disney and the state of Florida have reached a settlement agreement in their lawsuit about who controls Walt Disney World’s governing district.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, WGN 9

item.title: Activists form human chain across Nashville, Tennessee, on Covenant school shooting anniversary
item.description: Tennessee advocates for gun safety formed a three-mile human chain in Nashville on the anniversary of last year's deadly Covenant School shooting.
item.dc:creator: Travis Loller And Jonathan Mattise, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Hardy souls across New England and New York dig out after major winter storm
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Thousands of hardy souls across New England spent Sunday digging out after a major weekend storm dumped more than two feet of snow in some areas, caused multiple road accidents, downed power lines and left hundreds of thousands across the Northeast in the dark, some perhaps for days.
item.dc:creator: STEVE LeBLANC, Associated Press, WGN 9
